config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan dan merugikan orang kecil. Keseimbangan yang sehat harus melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as a lesser priority than society’s interests, because in the United States we have learned how easily “for the public good” can become an excuse for overreach. I do think there are cases where society must come first, like public health or safety, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and temporary. If individual rights are not protected first, then the majority can trample minorities and call it democracy. A healthy society is built by protecting people’s basic freedoms, not by assuming the collective always knows best.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, jelas dasarnya, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk merampas kebebasan dasar. Justru kalau hak individu tidak dilindungi, mayoritas atau aparat bisa semena-mena lalu menyebutnya sebagai kepentingan bersama. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically override individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily that logic can be abused by governments, majorities, or institutions. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; public health, safety, and preventing serious harm can justify limited restrictions when they are narrow and accountable. What I reject is the idea that the collective is inherently wiser or morally superior, because that mindset can silence minorities and excuse unfair treatment of people with less power. A free society has to prove that it can protect the common good without treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat praktik di Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering dirugikan justru orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi itu harus sempit, terukur, dan bisa diawasi ketat. Kalau hak individu tidak dipasang sebagai batas, kepentingan kolektif mudah berubah jadi alasan untuk menindas atas nama kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not sit above individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States we have plenty of history showing how “for the common good” can become a cover for abuse. I do think there are narrow exceptions for public health, safety, and preventing serious harm, but those exceptions have to be tightly limited and accountable. What I reject is the idea that the collective is automatically wiser or morally superior, because that mindset can flatten minorities and make it too easy to sacrifice people with less power. A healthy society protects the common good by respecting the individual first, not by treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
